protocol.name: gowl_input_capture_unstable_v1

description.summary: private input capture and injection for the gowl portal

protocol.description:
This is a gowl-private protocol used solely by the xdg-desktop-portal-gowl backend to implement the freedesktop InputCapture and RemoteDesktop portals on a gowl compositor.
The compositor owns the input pipeline directly, so unlike the layer-shell/pointer-constraints scheme used by the generic xdg-desktop-portal-wlr backend, this protocol lets the portal:
* enumerate capture zones derived from the monitor layout, * install pointer barriers on the outer boundary of the zone union, * be told when the cursor crosses a barrier (activation) and receive the captured pointer/keyboard event stream while active, and * inject pointer/keyboard events (RemoteDesktop) via the compositor's virtual-input devices.
It is intentionally NOT a public/stable protocol: it carries no security model of its own and trusts its single client (the portal), which the compositor launches. Warning! Backward-incompatible changes may be made; the 'z' prefix and version suffix are bumped accordingly.

interface.name: zgowl_input_capture_manager_v1
interface.description: The manager is the global the portal binds. It hands out capture sessions (egress) and injection objects (ingress).

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroying the manager does not affect sessions or injectors already created through it.

request.name: create_capture
request.description: Create a capture session used to enumerate zones, install pointer barriers, and receive captured input once a barrier is crossed.

request.name: create_inject
request.description: Create an injection object that drives the compositor's virtual pointer/keyboard for the RemoteDesktop portal.

interface.name: zgowl_input_capture_v1
interface.description: A capture session. After creation the client should request get_zones to learn the layout, set_barriers to install barriers, then enable. When the cursor crosses a barrier the compositor sends activated and begins streaming captured input events (rel_motion, button, axis, key, modifiers) until the session is deactivated.

entry.name: invalid_barrier
entry.summary: a barrier was not axis-aligned, had a zero id, or was not on the zone-union boundary

request.name: destroy
request.description: Releases the session, removing all barriers and ending any active capture.

request.name: get_zones
request.description: Ask the compositor to (re)send the current zone set via a series of zone events terminated by zones_done. The zone_set id in zones_done identifies this layout; barriers set against a stale zone_set are rejected.

request.name: add_barrier
request.description: Stage one axis-aligned pointer barrier. Barriers are not active until set_barriers is called. Horizontal barriers have y1 == y2, vertical barriers x1 == x2; id must be non-zero.

request.name: set_barriers
request.description: Validate and install the barriers staged with add_barrier against the given zone_set, replacing any previously installed set. The compositor replies with a barrier_status event per barrier, then a set_barriers_done event. Clears the staging list.

arg.name: zone_set
arg.summary: the zone_set the barriers are relative to

request.name: enable
request.description: Arm the session: once enabled, crossing an installed barrier activates input capture.

request.name: disable
request.description: Disarm the session. If currently active, capture is deactivated first (a deactivated event is sent).

request.name: release
request.description: End the current activation, restoring local input, optionally warping the cursor to (x, y). The session stays enabled so a later barrier crossing re-activates. Use has_position 0 to leave the cursor where it is.

event.name: zone
event.description: Part of a get_zones reply. Geometry is layout-relative; x/y may be negative.

event.name: zones_done
event.description: Terminates a get_zones reply and names the zone_set.

event.name: zones_changed
event.description: The monitor layout changed; the client should call get_zones again and reinstall barriers. Any installed barriers have been dropped.

event.name: barrier_status
event.description: Reports whether a barrier from the last set_barriers was accepted.

event.name: activated
event.description: The cursor crossed barrier barrier_id; capture is now active and captured-input events follow. cursor_x/cursor_y is the crossing point.

event.name: deactivated
event.description: Capture is no longer active; local input is restored.

event.name: disabled
event.description: The session was disabled (e.g. by the compositor).

event.name: button
event.description: Only sent while active. state is 1 for pressed, 0 for released.

event.name: axis
event.description: Only sent while active. axis 0 is vertical, 1 horizontal.

event.name: key
event.description: Only sent while active. state is 1 for pressed, 0 for released.

interface.name: zgowl_input_inject_v1
interface.description: Drives the compositor's virtual pointer/keyboard to emulate input for the RemoteDesktop portal. Each motion/button/axis/key request takes effect immediately.

request.name: pointer_motion_absolute
request.description: x/y are normalized [0,1] across the layout extents, matching the RemoteDesktop NotifyPointerMotionAbsolute convention.

request.name: button
request.description: state is 1 for pressed, 0 for released.

request.name: key

description.summary: inject a keyboard key by keycode

request.description: keycode is the evdev keycode (not offset by 8). state is 1 for pressed, 0 for released.

request.name: frame

description.summary: commit a batch of injected events

request.description: Groups the preceding inject requests into a single input frame.
